steps.title: Find the figurative bridge
steps.content: The claim requires figurative language that connects Washington, DC, with the rural South of the narrator's past. The quotation must refer to both places and link them imaginatively.

steps.title: Reject single-place quotes
steps.content: Choice A describes train sounds, Choice C gives a voice description, and Choice D describes trees on a Washington street; none connects the two places. The matching quotation has homeland soil falling like a fertile shower on Washington's streets. The answer is B.
